starry_unigraph.runtime.base module

Base runtime protocol and backward-compat adapter ABCs.

Defines:

  • RuntimeProtocol — minimal abstract interface for any graph computation paradigm (Flare DTDG, CTDG online, etc.).

  • RuntimeAdapter / ExecutionAdapter / GraphProvider — legacy abstract base classes used by providers.common.

class starry_unigraph.runtime.base.ExecutionAdapter[source]

Bases: ABC

abstract eval_step(runtime: RuntimeBundle, batch: Any) → dict[str, Any][source]
abstract predict_step(runtime: RuntimeBundle, batch: Any) → dict[str, Any][source]
abstract train_step(runtime: RuntimeBundle, batch: Any) → dict[str, Any][source]

Bases: ABC

abstract build_eval_iterator(session_ctx: SessionContext, split: str = 'val') → Iterable[Any][source]
abstract build_predict_iterator(session_ctx: SessionContext, split: str = 'test') → Iterable[Any][source]
abstract build_runtime(session_ctx: SessionContext) → RuntimeBundle[source]
abstract build_train_iterator(session_ctx: SessionContext, split: str = 'train') → Iterable[Any][source]
graph_mode = 'base'
abstract prepare_data(session_ctx: SessionContext) → PreparedArtifacts[source]
prepared: PreparedArtifacts | None

class starry_unigraph.runtime.base.RuntimeAdapter[source]

Bases: ABC

abstract dump_runtime_state(runtime: RuntimeBundle) → dict[str, Any][source]
abstract init_model(session_ctx: SessionContext) → Any[source]
abstract init_optimizer(session_ctx: SessionContext, model: Any) → Any[source]
abstract load_runtime_state(runtime: RuntimeBundle, state: dict[str, Any]) → None[source]
class starry_unigraph.runtime.base.RuntimeProtocol[source]

Bases: ABC

Minimal runtime interface for all graph computation paradigms.

Implementations should provide batch iteration, a single-step forward/backward, and state serialization.

Example subclass usage:

class MyRuntime(RuntimeProtocol):
    def iter_batches(self, split, **kw):
        for batch in self.loader(split):
            yield batch
    def step(self, batch, training):
        loss = self.model(batch)
        if training:
            loss.backward()
        return {"loss": float(loss)}
    def state_dict(self):
        return self.model.state_dict()
    def load_state_dict(self, state):
        self.model.load_state_dict(state)
abstract iter_batches(split: str, **kwargs: Any) → Iterable[Any][source]
abstract load_state_dict(state: dict[str, Any]) → None[source]
abstract state_dict() → dict[str, Any][source]
abstract step(batch: Any, training: bool) → dict[str, Any][source]
